point_reviews.reason: The document lists dry cough, shortness of breath, fatigue, and loss of taste or smell as common symptoms.
The document mentions other symptoms including fever, chills, headaches, body aches, and digestive issues.
The document states that symptoms can appear 2 to 14 days after exposure.
The document specifies severe symptoms requiring emergency help, including trouble breathing and persistent chest pain.
The document indicates that some individuals may experience symptoms worsening around 5 to 7 days after onset.

The document confirms that children generally experience similar symptoms but with less severity compared to adults.
